notes: Public-source v0 database for EU AI Stack.
Edges marked fact_status=inferred are strategic dependency hypotheses, not confirmed supplier contracts.
This is built for Codex ingestion and future website generation.
Next stage: enrich node metadata, add company sizes/funding/status, and expand source coverage.

nodes.name: EU AI Stack
nodes.description: The first battlefield: EU AI companies, compute, cloud, chips, AI factories, software stack, regulation and opportunity signals.

nodes.name: AI Compute
nodes.description: GPU/HPC/accelerator capacity for training, fine-tuning and inference.

nodes.name: GPU / AI Accelerators
nodes.description: High-end AI chips and accelerators used for AI training/inference.

nodes.name: AI-Optimized HPC / Supercomputing

nodes.name: Cloud-Native AI Platform Layer
nodes.description: Kubernetes, serving, storage, registry and MLOps tooling that makes AI infrastructure usable.

nodes.name: Model Distribution / Registry
nodes.description: Platforms used to distribute, discover and reuse models/datasets.

nodes.name: LLM Serving Layer
nodes.description: Software for high-throughput, cost-efficient model serving.

nodes.name: Sovereign Cloud
nodes.description: Cloud services designed around sovereignty, data residency and control.

edges.reason: AI compute is a core layer of the EU AI stack.
AI Factories are the public compute and support backbone for EU AI startups/SMEs and users.
Sovereign cloud is a critical deployment/control layer for EU AI.
Regulation and compliance shape deployment of AI in the EU.
AI accelerators depend on semiconductor manufacturing and lithography supply chains.
Cloud-native and MLOps tooling determine AI infrastructure usability.
The Commission supports AI Factories through EuroHPC/JU policy and funding architecture.
The Commission's InvestAI facility supports AI Gigafactories.
The AI Act is the EU AI regulatory framework.
European Chips Act is an EU strategic policy node for semiconductor resilience.
EuroHPC JU provides access to computing time and support services offered by AI Factories.
European AI Office collaborates on AI Act / AI ecosystem coordination.
InvestAI supports up to five AI Gigafactories.
AI Gigafactories require large-scale advanced AI processors.
AI Gigafactories require high power capacity and energy efficiency.
AI Gigafactories are large-scale compute facilities requiring data center infrastructure.

opportunities.title: Cloud-native AI layer for EuroHPC AI Factories
opportunities.reason: AI Factories create compute capacity, but HPC systems are not naturally cloud-native. A layer that makes EuroHPC resources usable like cloud infrastructure could unlock startups/SMEs.

opportunities.title: European GPU allocation and observability broker
opportunities.reason: Compute is a critical red dependency. A broker/observability layer could help startups discover, reserve, route and optimize scarce GPU/HPC capacity across European providers.

opportunities.title: AI Act evidence and compliance automation for regulated AI
opportunities.reason: EU AI deployment will require evidence, governance, risk documentation and compliance workflows. This is accessible to a software startup and strongly relevant to France/EU.

opportunities.title: Secure sovereign RAG/document AI for public-sector workflows
opportunities.reason: European public institutions and regulated companies need private GenAI over sensitive documents, with sovereignty and traceability. French positioning is strong through LightOn, Orange, AI2F and public programs.

opportunities.title: Model supply-chain security scanner for AI hubs and enterprises
opportunities.reason: Model registries and open model reuse create supply-chain risk. A scanner that checks licenses, provenance, unsafe serialization and known risk patterns could be useful for enterprises and public AI deployments.

opportunities.title: EU inference cost optimizer and routing layer
opportunities.reason: Inference cost and latency will become a bottleneck for enterprises deploying AI. A vLLM/TGI/Kubernetes-based optimizer could run across European GPU/cloud/HPC providers.

opportunities.title: AI data center energy/cooling intelligence for Europe
opportunities.reason: AI Gigafactories and AI clouds depend on power capacity, cooling and energy efficiency. Software monitoring/optimization around power and cooling has B2B value and sovereignty relevance.

opportunities.title: European language/domain data factory
opportunities.reason: Europe has many languages and domain-specific public/industrial data. A secure data curation/evaluation pipeline for EU languages and sectors can serve specialized model builders.

opportunities.title: Startup access layer for AI Factories
opportunities.reason: AI Factories offer compute and support, but startups need discovery, application assistance, usage tracking and workload packaging. Build the front door for the compute network.

opportunities.title: Sovereign AI dependency dashboard for VCs/corporates/public agencies
opportunities.reason: The graph itself can become a B2B product: dependency visibility, opportunity extraction and strategic positioning for investors, industrial groups and policy actors.

opportunities.title: Defense-adjacent secure AI deployment stack
opportunities.reason: Companies like Helsing show defense AI is software-native. A civilian-compliant secure AI deployment layer can later cross into defense-adjacent regulated environments.

opportunities.title: AI chip dependency intelligence for European procurement
opportunities.reason: European AI builders are exposed to US accelerators and global foundry bottlenecks. A procurement/dependency intelligence tool can help buyers plan alternatives and risk exposure.
